stories.title: All-Star Phase 1 Results — Ohtani and Clement Earn Automatic Starts
stories.angle: Phase 1 of 2026 All-Star voting closes at noon ET on June 25. With Shohei Ohtani leading all of baseball with 2,310,735 votes and Ernie Clement (Blue Jays) leading the American League with 2,054,130 votes, the top vote-getter in each league at each position earns an automatic starting spot. Phase 2 voting begins June 29 where the remaining spots are determined. The All-Star Game is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park in Philadelphia.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
All-Star Phase 1 voting closes TODAY at noon ET.

Leading the way:
— Ohtani: 2.31M votes, tops all of baseball
— Clement (Blue Jays): 2.05M, leads the AL
— Schwarber: 1.54M, leads the DH vote

Top vote-getter in each league earns an automatic start. #MLB #AllStarGame

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Ernie Clement (Blue Jays) leads the entire AL in All-Star voting — 80,000 votes ahead of Yordan Alvarez.

Blue Jays fan base showed up.

Phase 2 begins June 29. The full roster picture comes together ahead of July 14 in Philly. #BlueJays #MLB

facebook.long_form:
All-Star voting Phase 1 closes at noon today. Here's where things stand.

Shohei Ohtani leads all of baseball with 2,310,735 votes. He will earn an automatic starting spot in the National League. It is not close.

In the American League, the top vote-getter is Ernie Clement of the Toronto Blue Jays, who sits at 2,054,130 votes — approximately 80,000 votes ahead of Yordan Alvarez of the Houston Astros. That margin is genuinely surprising, and it speaks to how effectively Blue Jays fans can mobilize around a player they love. Clement's automatic AL starting spot is his. Unless Alvarez closes a gap of 80,000 in the next few hours, the second base spot is settled.

Kyle Schwarber leads the DH vote with 1,540,000 votes nationally. The All-Star Game is at Citizens Bank Park — his home stadium — on July 14. The entire city of Philadelphia is invested in this ballot.

Phase 2 begins June 29, where the top vote-getters at each position in each league from Phase 1 compete for the remaining starting spots on the All-Star roster. The final rosters are typically announced in early July.

All-Star Week in Philadelphia. July 14. Home Run Derby on July 13. Both events at one of baseball's great parks.

#MLB #AllStarGame #AllStarVoting
[Posting Window: 8:00 AM ET]

stories.title: Mike Trout on IL — Hamstring Injury Interrupts His Strongest Season in Years
stories.angle: Angels outfielder Mike Trout was placed on the 10-day IL on June 18 with a right hamstring strain suffered while running to first base against the Arizona Diamondbacks. Trout was having one of his strongest recent seasons before the injury, raising hopes of a full comeback year. He is upbeat but has no specific return timeline. One of baseball's all-time greats sidelined during a career-resurgence campaign.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Mike Trout is on the 10-day IL with a right hamstring strain.

He was having one of his strongest recent seasons. Then he strained it running to first base against the Diamondbacks.

No specific return date. But he says he felt "significantly better" the next day. #Angels #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Mike Trout. Hamstring. 10-day IL.

At a certain point the injury history stops being bad luck and starts being part of the story.

He was healthy and producing. He deserves a full year. The Angels need him back. #Angels

facebook.long_form:
On June 18, the Angels placed Mike Trout on the 10-day injured list with a right hamstring strain.

He'd been having one of his best recent seasons. For a player whose career since 2021 has been defined largely by injuries — and the what-ifs that come with them — 2026 was starting to look like the year the frustration ended. Then he ran to first base in a game against the Arizona Diamondbacks and felt the strain.

Trout was characteristically upbeat the next day, saying he felt "significantly better." There is no specific return date, but the optimism suggests he won't miss long. The 10-day IL minimum would put his earliest possible return around June 28-30.

Even a short absence matters for Trout. He is 34 years old. Every game he doesn't play is one fewer game on the leaderboard, one fewer chance to remind the world that when healthy, he remains one of the best players on the planet.

The Angels haven't contended in years. Trout hasn't had a full healthy season since 2019. In some ways, this hamstring strain — minor in itself — represents the broader theme of a generation of Trout seasons: brilliance interrupted, potential glimpsed but rarely realized over a full year.

He'll be back. The question is always whether he can stay back.

#Angels #MikeTrout #MLB
[Posting Window: 10:30 AM ET]

stories.title: Schwarber's Pace: 29 HR in 74 Games — What History Actually Says
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 29 home runs in 74 games, slashing .324/.439/.632 with 7 HRs in his last 18 June games alone. The Home Run Derby is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park — his home stadium. He has still not committed to participating. At his current pace, Schwarber would finish with approximately 64 home runs — a total fewer than five players in MLB history have ever reached.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber: 29 HRs in 74 games.

At that pace over 162: ~63 home runs.

The 60+ HR club in MLB history has fewer members than one hand.

The Derby is at Citizens Bank Park July 14. He still hasn't said yes. #Phillies #HomeRunDerby

facebook.long_form:
Let's do the math.

Kyle Schwarber has 29 home runs in 74 games. That rate, projected over a full 162-game season, puts him at approximately 63-65 home runs.

Take a moment to appreciate how rare that is. Sixty home runs in a season has been reached fewer times in Major League Baseball history than there are fingers on one hand. Roger Maris. Babe Ruth. Mark McGwire. Sammy Sosa. Barry Bonds. These are the names in the 60+ club. This is not a normal accomplishment.

Schwarber in June has been hitting .324 with a .439 on-base percentage and a .632 slugging percentage — seven home runs across 18 games. He is not slowing down.

The Home Run Derby is July 14. At Citizens Bank Park. His home park, in front of his home fans. The entire city of Philadelphia is watching to see if he'll participate.

His answer so far: "We'll see."

The argument for participating is obvious — it would be one of the great Home Run Derby moments in the event's history. The argument against is that swings in the Derby can sometimes disrupt a batter's mechanics.

Either way, the pace he is on is historic. Whether he enters the Derby or not, Schwarber is building one of the great power-hitting seasons in baseball's modern era. Twenty-nine home runs in June. Whatever comes next, this is already something to talk about.

#Phillies #KyleSchwarber #HomeRunDerby #MLB
[Posting Window: 12:30 PM ET]

stories.title: Bobby Witt Jr. Available Off Bench — Return to Starting Lineup Imminent
stories.angle: Royals shortstop Bobby Witt Jr. was available off the bench in Kansas City's June 24 game after missing five straight games with a Grade 1 MCL sprain in his right knee. Manager Matt Quatraro has said the Royals are "hopeful that in the next couple of days, he's available" to return to the full starting lineup. Witt was leading the AL with a 4.5 fWAR before the injury and is the biggest All-Star Game question mark remaining on the ballot.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Bobby Witt Jr. was available off the bench last night.

Manager: "Hopeful that in the next couple days, he's available" for the starting lineup.

The AL's fWAR leader (.294 / 10 HR / 28 SB / 4.5 fWAR) is almost back. #Royals #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Good news from Kansas City.

Bobby Witt Jr. was available off the bench last night, according to Royals manager Matt Quatraro — the clearest sign yet that his Grade 1 MCL knee sprain is healing on schedule.

Witt has been out since June 18, when he suffered the injury against the St. Louis Cardinals. He's missed five consecutive games. For a team trying to stay competitive, losing their best player — the AL's fWAR leader at 4.5 entering the injury — has been a meaningful blow.

But Quatraro's language has been consistently optimistic. "Hopeful that in the next couple of days, he's available" for the starting lineup is about as clear a return signal as a manager gives before it's officially announced.

Consider what's at stake beyond the win-loss column. Witt entered the injury hitting .294 with 10 home runs, 32 RBIs, 28 stolen bases, and a .294 batting average. He has been, by advanced metrics, the most valuable position player in the American League this season. His absence has been felt.

The All-Star Game is July 14. The ballot closes soon. Witt's return to the starting lineup before then matters for his All-Star visibility — and for a Royals team that needs their best player if they're going to make any kind of run in the second half.

He's almost back. That's the most important sentence of the day in Kansas City.

#Royals #BobbyWittJr #MLB
[Posting Window: 3:00 PM ET]

stories.title: Skubal Gives the Tigers a Trade Deadline Warning
stories.angle: Detroit Tigers ace Tarik Skubal issued a blunt deadline warning on June 23: "The reality is we need to play better baseball or else, come the deadline, you give the front office an option to reassess where this team is." The Tigers are 30-44 and in last place, 9.5 games behind the division leader. Skubal is a two-time AL Cy Young winner and a free agent after the 2026 season. His quote crystallizes the tension that every contender in baseball is watching: Detroit's front office will have to decide whether to sell the best pitcher in the AL.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal on the trade deadline:

"We need to play better baseball or else, come the deadline, you give the front office an option to reassess where this team is."

Tigers are 30-44. He's a two-time Cy Young and a free agent after 2026. #Tigers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Tarik Skubal said the quiet part out loud.

On June 23, the Detroit Tigers ace offered a remarkably direct assessment of his team's situation heading toward the August 3 trade deadline: "The reality is we need to play better baseball or else, come the deadline, you give the front office an option to reassess where this team is."

Translation: play better, or they might trade me.

The Tigers are 30-44. They're in last place in their division, 9.5 games behind the leader. The window for Detroit to reverse course and keep Skubal is narrowing week by week.

Skubal is a two-time AL Cy Young Award winner. He is also a free agent after the 2026 season, which means every team that trades for him is renting him for two months — and that two months could be the difference in a World Series run. The Braves, Blue Jays, Cubs, Dodgers, Padres, and Yankees have all been linked to him by reporters covering the deadline market.

For the Tigers, the calculus is painful: trade their best player for a prospect haul and begin a genuine rebuild, or hold on through a poor record in the hope that Skubal's warning jump-starts a turnaround.

His quote was not an accident. A two-time Cy Young winner doesn't issue a message like that without knowing exactly what he's saying.

Forty days to August 3. The Tigers are running out of time to make him take it back.

#Tigers #TarikSkubal #TradeDeadline #MLB
[Posting Window: 6:00 PM ET]
